title: cypripedium calceolus subsp. parviflorum
intro: Cypripedium calceolus subsp. parviflorum, also known as the small-flowered lady's slipper, is a rare and endangered orchid species found in the mountainous regions of North America and Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Cypripedium calceolus subsp. parviflorum are said to include reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of respiratory conditions, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as promoting wound healing and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a decoction or tincture, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and the need for proper identification.
